title: arenaria capillaris var. glabrata
intro: Arenaria capillaris var. glabrata, commonly known as Hair-stalked Sandwort or Glabrous Sandwort, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Arenaria capillaris var. glabrata include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
